nameMeanings.name: Bett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): from the Middle English personal name Bett(e). When feminine it was usually a pet form of Beatrice but possibly also Elizabeth. When masculine it was a pet form of Bertram or Bertelmew (see Bartholomew ).

variants.name: Betts
meanings: English: variant of Bett , with excrescent -s, a patronymic or metronymic from the medieval personal name Bett, a short form of Bartholomew, Beatrice, or Elizabeth.

variants.name: Bartholomew
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Bertilmew, Bertelmy, a borrowing of the Old French form of the Biblical personal name Bartholomew (from Latin Bartholomaeus; Hebrew ‘son of Talmai’, said to mean ‘having many furrows’, i.e. rich in land). This was an extremely popular personal name in Christian Europe, with many vernacular derivatives. It derived its popularity from the apostle Saint Bartholomew (Matthew 10:3), who was, among other things, the patron saint of tanners, vintners, and butlers.
As an Irish name, it has been used as an Anglicized form of Mac Pharthaláin (see McFarlane ).

variants.name: Betty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Francois, Jean-Paul.
English: from a diminutive of the Middle English personal name Bett(e) (see Bett ).
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Betto, an assimilated form of Berto (see Bert ).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.description: On April 24, 1638, a company of five-hundred English Puritans led by the Reverend John Davenport and Theophilus Eaton, a wealthy London merchant, sailed into the harbor.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Bett was born in 1615, his father, John Betts of Bearsted, Kent, was 39 and his mother, Mary, was 35. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1688, in Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 73.
